mnemonics: LDA Loves Shared Dance Moves (common covariance), QDA Quirks Demand Alone-time (individual covariances)
keyPoints: LDA assumes a common covariance matrix for all classes, which simplifies the model.
QDA uses a separate covariance matrix for each class, allowing for more flexibility.
The choice between LDA and QDA is a bias-variance trade-off: LDA is less flexible with lower variance, while QDA is more flexible with higher variance.
commonMistakes: Assuming LDA and QDA require data to be normally distributed; they assume class-conditional distributions are normal.
Using QDA when the number of observations in each class is small, as estimating individual covariance matrices requires sufficient data.
Applying LDA when the true class covariance structures are significantly different, leading to poor performance.
